question_stem:
Ken is mixing mortar to build a wall.
To build a 1-metre length of wall, he needs 10 kg of cement and 30 kg of sand.

Ken has one 25 kg bag of cement and one 100 kg bag of sand.
question_text: Work out the maximum length of wall Ken can build with the cement and sand that he has.
markscheme:
P1 for a process to find the length possible with one ingredient, e.g. $25 \div 10$ (= 2.5) or $100 \div 30$ (= 3.33...)

P1 for a complete process to find both possible lengths

A1 for 2.5 (m) or $2 \frac{1}{2}$ (m) with a correct justification or supporting calculations
